summary: AI Intelligence / recruiter scoring never appears for any email in production — hardcoded null/zero at the API layer — even though a real Gmail account is connected and the underlying emailAgent 'insights' mode works correctly when invoked directly.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 at https://5cb5f0620.abacusai.cloud/login and navigate to /dashboard/email.
2. Confirm the Inboxes bar shows 'melbvicduque@gmail.com — Primary' (Gmail IS connected).
3. Click the 'All Recruiter' tab to see the 64 real synced messages.
4. Click any of the first 5 email cards.
5. Observe the 'AI Intelligence' panel and the score badge on the card/detail header.
6. Repeat for a fresh browser session (session2) — identical result.
7. (Backend corroboration) GET /workspaces/emails/inbox returns "score":0 and "intelligence":null for literally every message, unconditionally (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py L389/L393).

8. (Agent corroboration) POST /agents/email/run {"mode":"insights","thread_id":"c075a8f1ba482d194fc8a0d83"} returns a REAL, non-fixture LLM score/breakdown/summary via the emailAgent, proving the capability works but is never called by the Email Center UI or its backing endpoint.
expected: Per the wireframe (email-center.html #ai-intel-em33) and the emailAgent's documented 'insights' mode, each recruiter email should show a real Interview Conversion Score, a 4-metric breakdown, and a narrative summary — and the copy should never claim Gmail isn't connected when it is.
observed: Every one of 64+ real synced threads shows a 0-score badge and 'No intelligence available yet — connect your Gmail account to enable AI scoring on your real threads.' — even though Gmail IS connected. GET /workspaces/emails/inbox hardcodes score:0 and intelligence:null for all messages; the endpoint never calls the emailAgent. Calling POST /agents/email/run mode=insights directly proves the agent itself works and returns real output (score 20, breakdown, summary, run_id c2037df705848d6adfe1e44a3, model deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro, tokensIn 415/tokensOut 203) — this is a pure UI/endpoint wiring gap, not an agent failure.

summary: AI Draft Reply / Send-Reply gate is completely unreachable from the UI for every one of 66+ real and freshly-created threads across two independent fresh sessions — draftReply is hardcoded to an empty string at the API layer, so the 'Send Reply' button and send-confirmation gate never render, and the UI instead shows the misleading fixed copy 'No reply needed for this email.' for every single thread.
reproduction: 1. Log in and go to /dashboard/email, click 'All Recruiter'.
2. Click through every one of the 64-66 visible email cards (scripted, both fresh sessions).
3. For each, check for [data-testid="draft-reply"] / [data-testid="open-send-gate-btn"].
4. Result: 0 of 65 cards (session1) and 0 of 66 cards (session2) ever show the draft-reply panel or Send Reply button; all show 'No reply needed for this email.'

5. (Backend corroboration) GET /workspaces/emails/inbox hardcodes "draftReply": "" for every message (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py L394); the frontend's own guard is `{selected.draftReply ? :

No reply needed for this email.

}` (apps/web/src/app/dashboard/email/page.tsx L557).

6. (Agent corroboration) POST /agents/email/run {"mode":"draft_reply","thread_id":"c075a8f1ba482d194fc8a0d83"} returns a real, resume-grounded, non-fixture draft ('Thank you for advancing my application for the Insights Analyst role. I will complete the assessments as instructed.', run_id ca64b35113ad514c79dace495) — proving the emailAgent itself works; the Email Center page never calls /agents/email/run at all (grep confirms zero references).

7. Because the Send Reply button can never render, the two-step send-confirmation gate specified in the wireframe (email-center.html #send-gate-em48) is likewise never reachable through the UI, for any thread, regardless of Gmail-connection state.
expected: Per the wireframe, a real AI-drafted reply (grounded in resume + thread, Voice-DNA scored) should appear for recruiter emails that warrant a reply, with Send/Edit/Preview/Regenerate actions leading into the two-step send-confirmation gate.
observed: Draft reply never appears for ANY thread (0/65 and 0/66 across two fresh sessions); the UI's honest-sounding 'No reply needed for this email.' copy is shown universally, implying an AI judgement that was never actually made — the field is simply always empty by construction, not by AI decision. Consequently the Send-Reply confirmation gate (the screen's headline safety feature per its own subtitle 'Every outbound reply passes the send confirmation gate') is structurally unreachable from the UI today.

summary: The default landing tab ('Priority') is permanently empty for every user because thread classification is never populated in production — a paying user opens Email Center to 'No emails in this view' despite 64+ real synced messages, and must know to manually click 'All Recruiter' to see anything.
reproduction: 1. Log in, navigate to /dashboard/email.
2. Observe the Smart Inbox panel loads with the 'Priority' tab pre-selected (component default state).
3. Observe '[data-testid="inbox-empty"] No emails in this view.' is shown.
4. Click 'Follow-Up Due', 'Auto-Replied', 'Trashed' — each is also empty.
5. Click 'All Recruiter' — 64+ real messages appear.

6. (Backend corroboration) every message's `classification` column is null; GET /workspaces/emails/inbox defaults `"category": t.get("classification") or "all"` so nothing can ever match "priority"/"followup"/"auto"/"trashed" — those 4 of 5 tabs are permanently dead ends because no triage ever runs against real data.
7. Reproduced identically in session1 and session2 (fresh browser contexts).
expected: Real recruiter emails should be triaged into Priority/Follow-Up/Auto-Replied categories (per the emailAgent's documented 'triage' mode and the wireframe's default Priority-tab view), so a paying user's first view of their inbox shows their most important emails, not an empty state.
observed: Priority/Follow-Up Due/Auto-Replied/Trashed tabs show '0 cards / No emails in this view' 100% of the time; only 'All Recruiter' (category='all' catch-all) ever shows content. A workaround exists (click All Recruiter) so this is HIGH rather than BLOCKER, but the default first-impression experience is a broken-looking empty inbox.

summary: The Smart Inbox column has no internal scroll container, pagination, or virtualization — with 64+ real threads it renders to ~8840px tall (whole page ~9138px), breaking the wireframe's fixed-height 3-column layout with an internally-scrolling email list.
reproduction: 1. Log in, go to /dashboard/email, click 'All Recruiter'.
2. Take a full-page screenshot — the Smart Inbox column stretches far below the other two columns.

3. Programmatic check: `getComputedStyle(document.querySelector('[data-testid=smart-inbox]'))` returns `overflowY: 'visible'`, `maxHeight: 'none'`, `height: '8840px'`; `document.body.scrollHeight` = 9138px.

4. Compare to wireframe (email-center.html #inbox-em12): `class="... flex flex-col"` with the email-list div `class="flex flex-col gap-2.5 overflow-y-auto pr-1"` inside a fixed-viewport-height column.
expected: Smart Inbox should be a fixed-height panel (matching the other two columns) with its own internal scrollbar, per the wireframe.
observed: No max-height/overflow constraint exists on the Smart Inbox section; the panel — and therefore the whole page — grows unbounded with the number of threads.

summary: 'This Week's Email Stats' and 'Automated Follow-Ups' panels are permanently hardcoded to zero/empty at the API layer regardless of real inbox activity, and the Follow-Ups empty-state copy also incorrectly tells an already-connected user to 'connect your Gmail account'.
reproduction: 1. Log in, go to /dashboard/email (Gmail already connected, 64+ real threads).
2. Observe 'This Week's Email Stats': Received=64 (real) but Recruiter=0, Auto-drafted=0, Sent (approved)=0, Follow-ups=0, Avg response=0h — all zero regardless of real content.
3. Observe 'Automated Follow-Ups' panel: 'No follow-ups queued yet — connect your Gmail account to enable the follow-up engine on real threads.' — shown even though Gmail IS connected.

4. (Backend corroboration) GET /workspaces/emails/inbox hardcodes `"recruiterEmails": 0, "autoDrafted": 0, "sentApproved": 0, "followUpsSent": 0, "avgResponseHrs": 0` and `"followUps": []` unconditionally (apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py L430-438).
expected: Stats should reflect real inbox activity once emails are synced/triaged; empty-state copy should never claim Gmail is disconnected when an account is actively connected.
observed: Stats and follow-ups are always zero/empty by construction, and the copy is misleading for an already-connected account.

summary: Multiple controls specified in the wireframe are entirely absent from the production Email Center — not degraded, not empty-stated, simply not built: 'Trash Automated/Spam', 'Mark All Read', 'Bulk Label' smart-actions; the Sort control (Interview Score/Career Potential/Recency); the 'Show thread history' expander; the Enthusiasm/Formality/Detail tone-breakdown bars; and the fixed bottom automation-status bar (Last scan/Next scan/Auto-reply mode toggle/Follow-up engine/Spam filter counter).
reproduction: 1. Compare design/screens/email-center.html against apps/web/src/app/dashboard/email/page.tsx and the live DOM at /dashboard/email.

2. grep page.tsx for: 'Trash Automated' (0 matches), 'Mark All Read' (0), 'Bulk Label' (0), 'Sort:' (0), 'thread history' (0), 'Enthusiasm'/'Formality' (0), 'Last scan'/'Next scan'/'Auto-reply mode'/'Spam filter' (0 each).

3. Visually confirm absence in test-artifacts/session1-01-initial-load.png and session1-04-email-selected-detail.png — no smart-action row above the category tabs, no sort row, no thread-history toggle under the email body, no tone sliders under the (non-rendering) draft panel, no fixed footer bar.
expected: Wireframe-specified controls should either be built, or — if intentionally descoped — the wireframe should be marked as such; currently a 3rd-party observer cannot tell these were descoped vs. simply broken.
observed: None of the listed controls exist anywhere in the shipped page.tsx or the live DOM.

summary: The 'LinkedIn Profile' link in the email-detail header is hardcoded to the generic https://www.linkedin.com/ homepage for every message, regardless of which sender/recruiter is selected — not a real per-sender deep link.
reproduction: 1. Select any email in the Smart Inbox.
2. Inspect the 'LinkedIn Profile' link in the detail header.
3. grep apps/web/src/app/dashboard/email/page.tsx for the anchor: `href="https://www.linkedin.com/"` is a literal constant, not derived from `selected`.
expected: The link should deep-link to the actual sender's LinkedIn profile (or be omitted/disabled when no such data exists), per the wireframe's per-recruiter 'Recruiter Profile' card intent.
observed: Same generic https://www.linkedin.com/ URL for every message, which reads as a working, sender-specific affordance but never is.
